title_first_work_inventory.title: 777 and Other Qabalistic Writings
priority_reasons: Liber number 777 in the work record.
The title is itself a symbolic number and the book is the clearest correspondence engine in the archive.

title_first_work_inventory.title: Liber AL vel Legis
priority_reasons: Liber number 220 in the work record.
The revealed source text anchors the whole system, so its title number is the first thing to read.
Liber titles deserve first-pass reading because Crowley uses the title as part of the number system.

title_first_work_inventory.title: Book 4 / Liber ABA
priority_reasons: Liber number 4 in the work record.
The title carries an explicit liber number and the work explains method as training.

title_first_work_inventory.title: Liber Aleph vel CXI
priority_reasons: The title carries a Roman-numeral signal that should be read as part of the work's symbolic identity.
Liber titles deserve first-pass reading because Crowley uses the title as part of the number system.

title_first_work_inventory.title: The Vision and the Voice
priority_reasons: Liber number 418 in the work record.
The work is one of the major visionary records and should stay high in the reading queue.

title_first_work_inventory.title: The Book of Thoth
priority_reasons: The tarot manual is the major late commentary on the revised symbolic system.

title_first_work_inventory.title: The Equinox, Vol. I, No. 1
priority_reasons: The serial title matters because Crowley uses publication structure as part of the doctrine.

title_first_work_inventory.title: Magick in Theory and Practice
priority_reasons: This is one of the clearest statements of Crowley's mature method and should be read alongside the number material.

title_first_work_inventory.title: The Book of Lies
priority_reasons: The title has no explicit numeral, but the compressed form makes it a high-value symbolic text.
93 is the core Thelemic number for Thelema and Agape.

tree_sources.name: Chesed
tree_sources.reason: Mercy. As Chesed, it defines a station of the Tree rather than a transit line, and the colors, names, and angelic attributions turn it into a structural node in the map. In the portal it reads as a place where doctrine, embodiment, and function meet. The entry keeps the sephirah visible as an active principle, not just a diagram label, so the reader can read it as part of the system's working anatomy.

priority_reasons: Liber Aleph vel CXI contains a Roman-numeral title signal that points to 111.

tree_sources.name: Path 11
tree_sources.reason: Path from Kether to Chokmah. The path binds Aleph to Air and The Fool, making the tarot attribution part of the explanation rather than a hidden assumption. The portal keeps the path as a working symbol of transition, relation, and symbolic progression. In the portal it functions as a route across the diagram, not just a line on a chart. That makes each path read as a practical bridge between states, symbols, and initiatory movement.

tree_sources.name: Path 22
tree_sources.reason: Path from Geburah to Tiphareth. The path binds Lamed to Libra and Adjustment, making the tarot attribution part of the explanation rather than a hidden assumption. Adjustment is a balancing path, and the note preserves Crowley's emphasis on measured correction rather than simple moral judgment. In the portal it functions as a route across the diagram, not just a line on a chart. That makes each path read as a practical bridge between states, symbols, and initiatory movement.
